Bitmap data for a 92x44 pixel "dead face" image used in the display driver.

# Purpose
The file defines a monochrome bitmap image using C language syntax. It specifies the dimensions of the image with `dead_face_width` and `dead_face_height`, which are set to 92 and 44, respectively. The `dead_face_bits` array contains the pixel data for the image, where each byte represents a series of 8 pixels in a row, with each bit indicating whether a pixel is on or off. This bitmap data is typically used in graphical applications to render a specific image, in this case, a "dead face" icon, on a display or within a user interface. The file is relevant to the codebase as it provides the necessary data to display this specific image within the application.
# Content Summary
The provided content is a C header file that defines a bitmap image. The file specifies the dimensions of the image and contains the bitmap data in a static array. The image is defined with a width of 92 pixels and a height of 44 pixels, as indicated by the macros `dead_face_width` and `dead_face_height`.

The bitmap data is stored in the `dead_face_bits` array, which is an array of unsigned characters. Each element in this array represents a portion of the image in a binary format. The data is organized in a way that each bit corresponds to a pixel in the image, with the entire array representing the complete image.

This file is typically used in graphical applications where the image needs to be rendered on a display. The bitmap data can be processed by a graphics library or a custom rendering function to display the image as intended. Developers working with this file should understand how to interpret the bitmap data and integrate it into their graphical rendering pipeline.
